g.text: OUTLAW OPERATIONS DIRECTIVE
A-CELL // FLASH PRECEDENCE // DESTROY AFTER READING
OP NAME:
FALSE ALARM
DTG:
[TONIGHT] 2100Z
HANDLER:
█████ ("PROVOST")
EXPECT:
3–4 HRS
COVER:
JOINT FAA / NWS FIELD TEAM
1. SITUATION
At 0312 local, FAA long-range radar Site ME-07 ("SABLE
RIDGE"), an automated station in the North Maine Woods,
transmitted a CORRUPTED DATA LOOP and went dark. It has
not answered remote reboot. Aroostook County Deputy
MARCUS VANCE responded at approx. 0500 and has not
checked in. We are watching this because the pre-crash
frames logged a contact that is not consistent with any
aircraft.
2. MISSION
Proceed to Site ME-07 and, in order of priority:
a. ERASE all unnatural data — on-site log drive AND
the FAA remote cloud backups.
b. RECOVER or TERMINATE Deputy Vance.
c. SECURE the facility.
d. COVER the incident as a catastrophic severe-weather-
generator failure.
3. EXECUTION & ROE
You are FAA/NWS on paper. The county has been told a
specialist team is inbound; the substation will not
interfere. Weapons discreet. NO local casualties that
cannot be explained by weather or wildlife. If any Agent
is compromised by what is on that ridge, the mission
takes precedence over the Agent. You know what that means.
4. SIGNAL / COMMS
Cellular is dead past the Notch. Encrypted VHF, callsign
OUTLAW-6. If your radios begin to answer you,
STOP TRANSMITTING. Weather window closes at dawn.
5. ATTACHMENTS
A — Site fact sheet (ME-07) C — County Ledger, wk.
B — Regional map D — Pattern of activity
E — Persons of interest (Vance / Rostova)
GREEN BOX
EYES ONLY
